mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.5 percent signals weakening labor market demand and rising recession probability, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power during non-recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and supply-side bottlenecks, which compress profit margins and trigger short-term S&P 500 drawdowns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60 percent of cases, or the signal shows no regime-dependent directional shift.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent establishes a zero lower bound environment that suppresses bond yields and forces institutional capital into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not significantly outperform Treasuries when FEDFUNDS stays below 0.25 percent, or the signal reverses direction during liquidity crunches.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms genuine labor market tightening, which boosts consumer spending expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in over 50 percent of observations, or PAYEMS growth fails to predict equity direction when UNRATE is stable.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing housing starts signal renewed construction activity and upstream supply chain demand, which expands corporate revenue pipelines and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: HOUST increases are followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases, or the series shows no persistent predictive relationship with equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rebounds above 105 index points during post-shock recovery phases indicate restored manufacturing capacity and easing supply constraints, which trigger short-term S&P 500 rallies.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing above 105 during recovery phases fails to produce positive 5-day returns in at least 60 percent of instances, or the signal loses significance outside recovery regimes.
